title: Chronic pain resources - Canada.ca
content:
## Chronic pain resources

## Free virtual resources for chronic pain management are available through the Power Over Pain Portal.
- Provincial and territorial resources
- Indigenous resources
- For health care providers
- Canadian Pain Society
- Chronic Pain Network
- People in Pain Network
- Pain Management Resources (Arthritis Society Canada)
- Canadian pain clinic listing (McMaster University)

### Children, parents and caregivers
- Solutions for Kids in Pain
- Pediatric Pain Management Standard

### Mental health and substance use
- Get help with substance use
- Mental health support
- Substance use support for tradespeople

### Courses and education
- Chronic Health Conditions Course (Online Therapy Unit)
- Empowered Relief (Stanford University)
- Modules to manage your pain (Toronto Academic Pain Medicine Institute)

- Pain management and treatment (My Health Alberta)

- Provincial health services
- Chronic pain (Government of British Columbia)

- Provincial health services

- Provincial health services

## Indigenous resources
- Aboriginal Children's Hurt and Healing Initiative
- Chronic Condition Prevention and Management (First Nations Health Authority)
- Chronic Pain Toolkit (Grand Council Treaty #3)
- Chronic Pain and Indigenous Peoples (Pain BC)
- Pain, Pain Killers and Indigenous Peoples: Choose the right medicine for you in partnership with your physician (First Nations Health Authority)
- Which Medicines will Support my Recovery from Opioid Addiction? (Thunderbird Partnership Foundation)

### Hope for Wellness Help Line
Available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week to Indigenous people seeking emotional support, crisis intervention or referrals to community-based services.
Support is available in English and French, and in Cree, Ojibway and Inuktitut by request.
Phone: 1-855-242-3310 (toll-free)
Hope for Wellness online chat
- Chronic Pain Centre of Excellence for Canadian Veterans

## For health care providers
- Chronic Pain Care for Indigenous Patients: A Primer for Healthcare Providers (Centre for Wise Practices in Indigenous Health)
- 2022 Consensus Statement for the Prescription of Pain Medication at Discharge after Elective Adult Surgery (Taylor & Francis)
- 2025 Canadian Opioid Prescribing Guideline for the Management of Chronic Non-Cancer Pain (McMaster University) (PDF, 280 KB)
- Opioid Crisis curriculum (Association of Faculties of Medicine of Canada)
- American Society of Regional Anesthesia and Pain Medicine (ASRA)
- Evidence on pain management (Canada's Drug Agency)
- Annual Scientific Meeting (Canadian Pain Society)
- National Pain Rounds (Canadian Pain Society)
- Chronic non-cancer pain management and opioid resources (The College of Family Physicians of Canada)
- ECHO Chronic Pain & Opioid Stewardship Canada
- International Association for the Study of Pain
- The Journal of the International Association for the Study of Pain
- Canadian Journal of Pain
- Pain Foundations (Pain Canada)
- Moving Through Pain (Pain Canada)
